channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Grounded 2 Into The Abyss Will Feature New Armors, And Mysterious Underwater Exploration - Interview
item.content:encoded:
Grounded 2 is continuing its steady march towards 1.0. At the Summer Game Fest Showcase, we were treated to a trailer showing off the new "Into the Abyss" update, along with the release on PlayStation on August 5th.

The "Into The Abyss" update is a water-themed update that will expand player traversal options, add all kinds of new locations to explore and items to craft, and will be one of the first big injections of story into this Early Access title.

At Summer Game Fest this year, I got to go hands-on with the PlayStation 5 version of the game, getting thrown into the fight against King Dozer, a fire-spitting lizard from the Beat the Heat update. I then got to sit down with Game Director, Chris Parker, to talk about the PlayStation 5 release, what's coming in the future, and how player feedback has been shaping the continued development of Grounded 2.

To talk at a high level of the 'Into The Abyss' update releasing on August 11, Parker explained, "The Into The Abyss update has two key factors. The first factor is that we're coming out on PlayStation 5. We're super excited to have the PlayStation Community join our Early Access program and help provide feedback and thoughts about making the game."

"Also, just as exciting to us is that this is a huge content update. We're adding a huge chunk into the park that consists of three different biomes. A huge mountain biome that's cut with different little dungeon areas cutting through it. There's a huge park pond, and on the other side, there's this sort of toxic bayou. This marshy area is filled with its own challenges and hurdles. But you need to adventure through it because what you're needing to do in this expansion is adventure lower and lower into the pond to uncover a mystery at the bottom of the pond."

Talking about what new content players will be able to get their hands on and into their inventory, Parker added "There's a lot going on, new creatures, lots of new armor and systems that you'll need to interact with, and just overall I think it's shaping up to be our biggest and best update yet."

Knowing that Obsidian Entertainment is always marching towards that 1.0 release, I did ask if there was a known timeline to hitting 1.0.

"We have thought about how we would like 1.0 to be, but we're not talking about that just yet."

Because Grounded 2 is still in early access, there has been a focus on adding gameplay, but the story has been thin. Parker explained that the new areas experienced in Into The Abyss "are actually very important in the story, so when that's completed it will set the game up for more."

Most Anticipated Update Reactions

I asked Parker what key things from the update he was most looking forward to players getting their hands on. After pausing to think for a moment, he began, "There are two things I might talk about quickly. The first thing is we're excited to have our first amphibious buggy. I think players like and enjoy the buggies, and with this one, because it's amphibious, you're riding it like an ATV when you're on the ground, then you go into the water, it is more of a sleek mode, and you can cruise around. I think it feels great."

"The other thing, which we're really only teasing right now, but I'll talk to it anyway. At the end of the trailer that we put out, you can see Max in a very large area, and he's got on what appears to be a diving suit light."

There's a bottom of the lake experience where we are doing something new and different and amazing, I can't wait to do the full reveal

Grounded 2 On The PlayStation 5 And Continued Development

As Grounded 2 has been available in Early Access since July 2025, it has had a long chance to grow, and the team at Obsidian Entertainment has had plenty of chances to gather feedback from the player base. I asked how the reception had been and what they were looking forward to with PlayStation 5 players hopping in.

"I think it's interesting that the PlayStation 5 users will get the benefit of almost a year's worth of Early Access, where we've been gathering feedback and have been fixing things that the community has asked for. A couple of big changes are coming for 0.5 that the community is very excited about, like Zip Lines."

"Zip lines let you get around the park very quickly. We did not have them in Grounded 2 at Early Access because we wanted to make sure that the buggy experience was everything we wanted it to be." Here, Parker referred to the mount systems where riding ants and other creatures are called buggies, not any actual game bugs. "Now that we've sorted that out, it's time to bring zip lines back."

"We're also updating how brain power works, which we're tackling in a couple of different ways, and this is something people in the community have asked for. There's a host of things that we have done that are for the community."

Some non-community-driven changes that are going to be taking place with the Into The Abyss update are that the team went back and "looked at how the Garden quests were received vs how the Picnic quests were received and figured out what the community liked more. We used that to influence a lot of changes that we made to the beginning of the game so that it will be a stronger experience for everybody who either starts playing now on the existing platforms or joins up with us once we've added the PlayStation 5."

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew
